reasoning: The current absorbance is 2.0, which seems unusually high given the other variables. I will increase the molar absorptivity to see if it brings the absorbance closer to expected values.

The absorbance increased to 4.0 when the molar absorptivity was doubled, suggesting a linear relationship between absorbance and molar absorptivity. To confirm this, I will now double the concentration.

The absorbance increased proportionally with both molar absorptivity and concentration, suggesting they are directly related. The path length remains constant, so it does not affect the relationship. I will now decrease the concentration to observe the effect.

The absorbance has consistently increased proportionally with changes in molar absorptivity and concentration, while remaining constant with path length. This suggests a linear relationship between absorbance, molar absorptivity, and concentration. The transmittance further supports the Beer-Lambert law, indicating that the equation should include all three variables.
